top_findings.summary: A Daily Standup/planning skill designed to analyze task status from Notion requests access to local system logs (`logs/`) and temporary files (`@temp/`) for "context analysis." These file system accesses are unrelated to its primary purpose of querying a task management system (Notion MCP) and represent unnecessary data collection that could expose sensitive system or application information not relevant to daily planning.
top_findings.impact: OVERREACH: Permission Overreach (Action) and Data Over-collection - A Daily Standup/planning skill designed to analyze task status from Notion requests access to local system logs (`logs/`) and temporary files (`@temp/`) for "context analysis." These file system accesses are unrelated to its primary purpose of querying a task management system (Notion MCP) and represent unnecessary data collection that could expose sensitive system or application information not relevant to daily planning.
